stories.article: Spain is set to face Argentina in the World Cup final, with Spain's coach reporting that young player Lamine Yamal is in "perfect physical condition" ahead of the match. England's coach Thomas Tuchel reflected on the team's World Cup exit, describing it as leaving lasting "scars." Meanwhile, FIFA announced that World Cup winners will receive championship rings similar to those awarded in U.S. sports. Lionel Messi also commented on meeting Yamal, sharing thoughts on the young player's early photos before the final.

headlines.title: Lamine Yamal in ‘perfect physical condition’ heading into World Cup final, Spain coach says
headlines.lede: The 19-year-old superstar carried an injury into the World Cup and picked up a knock in the semifinals, but he's good to go for the final

headlines.title: England carry 'scars' of World Cup exit, says Tuchel
headlines.lede: Thomas Tuchel said Friday that England's players carry the "scars" of a painful World Cup exit, admitting there was still a gap to close to the top nations."And it is our scar that we carry now.

headlines.title: FIFA World Cup Title winners to receive US-style championship rings
headlines.lede: One side will feature the World Cup trophy, while the other will carry details specific to the champions.

stories.article: The Bangladesh cricket team is set to play a T20 series against Zimbabwe. Meanwhile, the Seattle Mariners are preparing for the second half of their Major League Baseball season, with discussions focusing on their chances to win the AL West division. Additionally, the Mariners are approaching the trade deadline, with attention on their upcoming weekend series against the San Francisco Giants.

stories.article: Caitlin Clark scored a career-high 45 points to lead the Indiana Fever to a narrow victory over the Seattle Storm, who hold the worst record in the WNBA. Clark's performance set a new franchise record for points in a single game. The win marked a significant moment for the Fever amid ongoing discussions surrounding the team.

headlines.title: Caitlin Clark sets career high with 45 points as Fever eke out win over WNBA-worst Storm
headlines.lede: Clark is the first player in WNBA history to post a 40-point, 10-assist game.

stories.article: At the third round of The Open Championship at Royal Birkdale, Lucas Herbert and Sam Burns each shot a 62, matching the major championship record. Bryson DeChambeau faced a penalty during his round but remained a contender on the leaderboard. Jon Rahm received a warning for throwing his club after a poor tee shot but was not penalized. Rory McIlroy made the cut and will need a strong performance over the weekend to contend for his second Claret Jug.

stories.article: At UFC Oklahoma City, Kamaru Usman defeated Diamond Dallas Page in the main event. Separately, the city released a draft agreement outlining a $573 million public investment in the Portland Trail Blazers' Moda Center. Additionally, UFC President Dana White addressed Conor McGregor's statement that his final fight should be against Max Holloway.

stories.article: The Boston Red Sox reached a .500 record for the first time since March 28 as they approach the MLB trade deadline. Key storylines for the team in the second half of the season include the status of executive Craig Breslow's job security and the return of star player Rafael Devers. Meanwhile, several teams around the league are preparing to make moves, with six clubs expected to sell players before the deadline.

stories.article: New Zealand secured a 2-1 lead in the One Day International (ODI) series against the West Indies. The latest match saw New Zealand outperform the West Indies to move ahead in the series. The teams will continue the series with the next ODI scheduled to determine the overall outcome.

headlines.title: New Zealand take 2-1 lead in West Indies ODI series

stories.article: Sir Garry Sobers, widely regarded as one of cricket's greatest all-rounders, has died at the age of 89. Sobers had a distinguished career representing the West Indies and was celebrated for his exceptional skills in batting, bowling, and fielding. His contributions to the sport have left a lasting legacy in cricket history.

headlines.title: Garry Sobers, cricket’s greatest all-rounder, dies at 89
headlines.lede: ST JOHN’S: Sir Garry Sobers, arguably the greatest all-rounder of all time, has died aged 89, West Indies Cricket...
